---
title: "HPC Spring School June 2026: SURF Research Cloud Workshop"
---

## Overview

Welcome to the SURF Research Cloud workshop for the UU HPC Spring School 2026!

During this hands-on workshop, you'll learn how to work effectively with SURF Research Cloud. There are several tutorials available for you to explore, covering key topics such as:

- **Working with R/Python environments**: Set up and manage environments in your workspace for reproducible research
- **Data transfer methods**: Move data to and from your workspace efficiently
- **Working with Ollama**: Learn to use large language models (LLMs)
- **Running long jobs**: Keep computational tasks running in the background 
- **VS Code Remote-SSH**: Connect your local VS Code to Research Cloud workspaces to edit files and run code remotely

Each tutorial includes step-by-step instructions. You can choose the one most relevant to your research needs or interests. While there are several workspaces available that you can use to follow the tutorials, we recommend using the [VRE Lab](../workspaces/programming/vre-lab.qmd) workspace for most of them, as it comes with many relevant tools pre-installed.

## Prerequisites

**Required**

Before starting the tutorials, you need:

1. **SURF Research Cloud account**: You can login here [**Research Cloud portal**](https://portal.live.surfresearchcloud.nl/)
2. **Active workspace**: You will need to create a workspace, depending on the tutorial you wish to follow. See [How to Create a Workspace](../first-steps.qmd#create-a-workspace) for guidance.

**Recommended**

Familiarity with the command line (terminal), git, GitHub and basic knowledge of Python/R will be helpful for some tutorials, but not required.

## Workshop Tutorials

### 1. Working with R/Python Environments

Learn how to create, manage, and use Python and R environments in your workspace. Working with environments is good practice for creating robust and reproducible projects, and makes it easier to install the exact same package versions that you are using on SURF Research Cloud.

**What you'll learn:**

- Creating virtual environments and managing packages
- Installing packages
- Best practices for reproducible research

**Tutorials:**

- [Working with Python Environments and Best Practices](../manuals/python-environment.qmd)

We recommend using the [VRE Lab](../workspaces/programming/vre-lab.qmd) workspace to follow this tutorial. 

- [Working with R Environments and Best Practices](../manuals/r-environment.qmd) 

We recommend using the [VRE Lab](../workspaces/programming/vre-lab.qmd) workspace to follow this tutorial. Select RStudioUU under optional components in the last step of creating your workspace.

**Estimated time:** 15-45 minutes (depending on any previous experience with environment management tools)

---

### 2. Data Transfer Methods

Transfer data between your local computer, cloud storage, Yoda, GitHub, and SURF Research Cloud workspaces. Learn which method works best for your data source and file sizes.

**What you'll learn:**

- Transferring files from your PC/laptop
- Using iBridges/iCommands for Yoda/iRODS data
- Connecting to SURFdrive and ResearchDrive
- Choosing the right transfer method for your needs

**Tutorials:**

To find the best method to transfer your data, check out this [Interactive Data Transfer Tool.](../manuals/data-transfer-options.qmd) It will help you determine the best method based on your data source, workspace, and file sizes.

You can also explore the following tutorials for specific transfer methods:

- [Data Transfer to and from Yoda using iBridges](../manuals/ibridges.qmd)
- [Data Transfer to and from Yoda using iCommands](../manuals/icommands.qmd)
- [Data Transfer from your computer using scp and rsync](../manuals/ssh-data-transfer-methods.qmd)
- [Data Transfer between Research Cloud and Online Storage (Surfdrive, Researchdrive) using Rclone](../manuals/rclone-researchcloud.qmd)
- [Data Transfer from GitHub/GitLab using Git](../manuals/git-clone.qmd)

**Estimated time:** 15-30 minutes (depending on tutorial selected)

---

### 3. Working with Ollama 

Set up and use [Ollama](https://ollama.com/) directly in your SURF Research Cloud workspace. Use it to run large language models (LLMs), automate several tasks, and chat with several models.

**What you'll learn:**

- Installing and configuring Ollama
- Integrating Ollama with your workflow

**Relevant Workspaces:** [VRE Lab](../workspaces/programming/vre-lab.qmd), [Python Workbench Desktop](../workspaces/programming/python-workbench.qmd)

**Tutorial:** [Setting up and Using Ollama](../manuals/ollama.qmd) 

**Estimated time:** 20-30 minutes

---

### 4. Running Long Jobs via the Command Line

Keep computational tasks running even after (accidentally) closing your terminal or SSH connection. Essential for long-running analyses and simulations.

**What you'll learn:**

- Running jobs in the background
- Checking job status and output
- Managing multiple long-running processes

**Tutorial:**

- [Running Long Jobs](../manuals/long-jobs.qmd)
This tutorial is relevant if you have an analysis script or simulation that takes a long time to execute. 

---

### 5. Connecting VS Code to Research Cloud

Connect your local Visual Studio Code to your SURF Research Cloud workspace. Edit files, run code, use local extensions, and debug directly from your local VS Code while files stay on the workspace.

**What you'll learn:**

- Setting up VS Code Remote-SSH extension
- Connecting to your workspace
- Editing files remotely
- Using VS Code extensions on the remote workspace

Works on any workspace with SSH access, however [VRE Lab](../workspaces/programming/vre-lab.qmd) is recommended. 

**Tutorial:**

- [VS Code Remote-SSH](../manuals/vscode-to-researchcloud.qmd)

**Estimated time:** 30 minutes

---
